compiled_state.title: Polyglot Lint & Format Gates: stack-agnostic lint/format config and gate scripts
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

The existing lint/format gates are Python-specific: auto-format.sh only handles .py files via ruff, validate.sh runs ruff check and mypy unconditionally. Ruby and JavaScript developers who run validate.sh see Python-specific output and no linting/formatting for their stack. This epic adds commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check keys to make the gate pipeline stack-agnostic. Kudos epic 01f5-28c1 (polyglot kudos triggers) T9 trigger reads commands.lint — until this epic ships, non-Python T9 always emits TRIGGER_SKIPPED.

## Success Criteria

- auto-format.sh reads commands.format; emits [DSO WARN] when absent with exit 0; when present, invokes configured formatter on staged files
- validate.sh reads commands.lint (new key alongside existing commands.lint_ruff / commands.lint_mypy); reads new key first, falls back to legacy; emits [DSO WARN] and skips when both absent
- gate-2d-dependency-check.sh and gate-2b-blast-radius.sh read commands.format_check; emit [DSO WARN] and skip when absent
- Onboarding writes stack-specific defaults: Python (ruff check / ruff --fix / ruff check), Node/JS (eslint --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern [introduced ESLint v6.8.0; not recommended for ESLint v10 flat config — use files array in config] / prettier --write / prettier --check), Ruby (bundle exec rubocop / bundle exec rubocop --autocorrect [safe corrections only, RuboCop v1.30+] / bundle exec rubocop)
- Re-onboarding preserves existing values (key-presence-based); emits [DSO INFO] commands.<key> already set — skipping per preserved key
- Validation: run onboarding on a Ruby project; validate.sh runs bundle exec rubocop instead of ruff

## Dependencies

Depends on Epic A (87e9-0bc8) — onboarding gate sequencing requires Epic A's commands.test_runner write to precede Epic B's writes

## Approach

Add three new config keys (commands.lint, commands.format, commands.format_check). Update auto-format.sh, validate.sh, gate-2d, and gate-2b to read these keys. Onboarding writes per-stack defaults using the same detection logic established by Epic A. New keys coexist with legacy commands.lint_ruff / commands.lint_mypy; validate.sh reads new key first, falls back to legacy.

## Research Findings

- Tool conventions: ruff (Python) covers both lint and format (subcommand modes); ESLint + Prettier (Node/JS/TS) are separate — ESLint lints, Prettier formats; RuboCop (Ruby) covers both via subcommands (`--autocorrect` for format, plain for lint)
- ESLint flag compatibility: `--no-error-on-unmatched-pattern` introduced in ESLint v6.8.0; explicitly documented as "not recommended for ESLint v10 flat config" — use `files` array in config instead
- RuboCop auto-correct safety: `--autocorrect` (v1.30+) applies only safe corrections by default; unsafe corrections require explicit `--autocorrect-all` — this epic uses safe mode only
- Key coexistence pattern: new commands.lint / commands.format / commands.format_check keys are additive alongside legacy commands.lint_ruff / commands.lint_mypy — validate.sh reads new key first, falls back to legacy (or emits DSO WARN with both absent)
- Onboarding key-preservation semantics: key-presence-based INFO signal ([DSO INFO] commands.<key> already set — skipping per preserved key) established by Epic A's onboarding pattern; re-onboarding doesn't overwrite user customizations

## Scenario Analysis

- Ruby project with both Gemfile and package.json at root (medium, detection): stack detection order from Epic A determines winner; onboarding writes whichever stack wins; documented behavior
- ESLint v10 project uses flat config and rejects legacy `--no-error-on-unmatched-pattern` (medium, compatibility): scaffolded default includes the flag; ESLint v10 users edit `files` array in config per note — acceptable operator burden
- RuboCop `--autocorrect` auto-applies safe fixes but user expects manual review (low, UX): safe-only mode limits damage; fixes can be reverted via git; documented behavior
- commands.format absent but auto-format.sh called during PostToolUse hook on .py edit (medium, regression): WARN + exit 0 prevents hook failure; existing Python .py edits continue via CLAUDE.md rule "Formatting runs automatically via PostToolUse hook" — fallback to ruff for Python-detected stack when key absent
- Onboarding writes stack-specific default but user has custom lint config (medium, deployment): key-presence-based skip preserves user customization; [DSO INFO] signal makes preservation observable
- Epic A (87e9-0bc8) sequencing broken — commands.test_runner writes AFTER commands.lint (high, dependency): onboarding phase ordering must enforce Epic A → Epic B write sequence; depends_on link ensures sprint scheduling order, but within onboarding the step order matters
- validate.sh runs `ruff check` unconditionally today — removing that requires backward-compat path (medium, regression): new commands.lint key coexists with legacy commands.lint_ruff; validate.sh reads new key first, falls back to legacy; no sudden behavioral change on upgrade

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (ESLint, Prettier, RuboCop CLIs with specific version caveats), migration/compatibility (ESLint v10 flat config vs legacy, RuboCop --autocorrect safety semantics v1.30+)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (onboarding SKILL.md Phase 3 Step 2b additions for Java branch; auto-format.sh, validate.sh, gate-2d, gate-2b behavioral changes)
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — per-commit gate invocation + per-onboarding write; not volume-sensitive
